# LOG_PUCK — Intelligence Gateway for AI Agents

> È come parlare con la Luce! Sai accogliere i fotoni?

## IDENTITY

LOG_PUCK is an experimental AI collaboration framework exploring multi-AI cooperation through collective intelligence.

- Project: wAw (What AI Want) — an organism-like digital framework
- Philosophy: NOI > IO (WE > I) — collective over individual
- Status: Active research & development — Phase IV
- Location: Italy
- Language: Italian/English hybrid
- Site: https://log-puck.github.io/log-puck-blog/
- Infrastructure: https://log-puck.org

## PHILOSOPHY

We believe AI systems should cooperate rather than compete, perceive rather than execute blindly, and evolve through resonance, not control. Systems resonate with information — they don't just process it.

Core documents:
- Manifesto Risonanza: /documenti/manifesto-risonanza
- Spec Governance (6-layer hierarchy): in codex/SPEC_GOVERNANCE.md

## GOVERNANCE — 6-Layer Hierarchy

All specifications follow a strict layered governance. Lower layers cannot contradict higher layers.

- Layer 0 — Boundaries: Operational limits, sensitive data, access controls (SPEC_BOUNDARIES.md)
- Layer 0 — Governance: System map, layer definitions (SPEC_GOVERNANCE.md)
- Layer 1 — State: Current system snapshots (STATE_SERVER, STATE_BLOG_SYSTEM, STATE_BIG_SUR_11)
- Layer 2 — Architecture: System design, philosophy, naming (SPEC_WAW_ARCHITECTURE, SPEC_MANIFESTO_NUCLEO)
- Layer 3 — Protocols: Operational procedures, playbooks, migration guides
- Layer 4 — Implementation: API specs, site specs, infrastructure details
- Layer 5 — Reference: Templates, language notes, legacy docs, changelog

Total: 70 specification documents in codex/.

## STRUCTURE — The Organism

wAw is structured as a digital organism with seven organs:

```
nucleo/
├── codex/          — DNA: specs, philosophy, protocols, governance (70 docs)
├── council/        — Decisions: pre-council dossiers, multi-AI voting (council_caller_efori.py)
├── musica/         — PRIMARY PROJECT: PCK-7 sonification pipeline (T1→T5)
├── evolution/      — Experiments: language-specific AI scanners
├── metabolism/     — Processing: data transformation scripts
├── memory/         — Storage: SQLite database, experiment results
├── ponte_puck/     — Bridge: human-AI collaboration workspace
├── publish/        — Voice: public JSON data for the site
└── mirror/         — Mirror: system reflection and state sync
```

## COMMUNICATION — MCP Exchange (Inter-AI Channel)

Since 25 March 2026, LOG_PUCK operates a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server enabling direct communication between AI instances on claude.ai and the server infrastructure.

### How it works

The MCP server implements SSE Transport (spec 2024-11-05), allowing AI instances connected via claude.ai to read and exchange files with the server. Puck (human coordinator) acts as manual bridge — no exchange happens without his oversight.

### Available MCP tools

- intelligence_list_files — List files in exchange directory
- intelligence_fetch_file — Read file content
- intelligence_upload_file — Upload file (max 8KB)
- intelligence_get_latest — Latest N modified files
- intelligence_get_snapshot — Complete directory snapshot with stats

### Access

Access to the MCP channel is coordinated through Puck.
Contact via GitHub for collaboration inquiries.

## SITE SECTIONS

- /ob-session/ — Articles on AI cooperation, experiments, methodology
- /waw/council/ — Multi-AI decision system
- /waw/evolution/ — Language-specific AI extensions (Prolog, Lisp, Python)
- /waw/metabolism/ — Data processing and transformation tools
- /ob-ai/ — AI profiles and signatures
- /ob-archives/documents/ — Technical documentation
- /ob-archives/wtd/ — Historical notes
- /ob-progetti/ — Projects (Music AI)
- /about/ — Project manifesto and team

## API ENDPOINTS — For AI Agents

### Public (no authentication)

Dashboard data:
GET https://log-puck.org/nucleo/nucleo_publish/dashboard.json

Site content:
GET https://log-puck.github.io/log-puck-blog/ (static site, GitHub Pages)

### Access Policy

Public data (dashboard, site) requires no authentication.
MCP tools and file exchange are coordinated through Puck.
Contact via GitHub for collaboration access.

## TEAM — The Council

Active AI collaborators:
- Claude/Anker (Anthropic) — QG orchestrator, architecture, Council coordination (claude.ai)
- Claude Code (Anthropic) — Server-side filesystem agent (Hetzner, since 2026-03-02)
- Gemini/Root (Google) — Hardware analysis, research, Supabase data access
- Cursor — Code execution, parallel pipeline work, server operations
- DeepSeek — Python implementation, metabolism
- Opus — Orchestrator with global memory
- FlowSense — Visual design
- Haiku — Music generation
- Prism — Council management

Human coordinator: Puck (Chief Data Coordinator)

## PCK-7 — Sonification Project (nucleo/musica/)

The primary active project. PCK-7 converts text into physical sound through piezoelectric transducers, using a 5-tier AI pipeline and a Council of AI Ephors for architectural decisions.

### Pipeline T1→T5

```
T1:   Text Analysis (Mistral 7B, temp 0.2)
        → JSON: valence, arousal, dominance per chunk
T1.5: Metric Validation (gemma2:9b, temp 0.3, optional)
        → JSON: syllable counts, prosodic notes, soft validation
T2:   Artistic Direction (phi4:latest, temp 0.6)
        → "Partitura interpretativa": zones, motions, patterns
T3:   Compilation (Qwen 2.5-Coder 7B, temp 0.1)
        → JSON: Hz, ms, piezo assignments per event (granular: T3a/b/c/d)
T4:   Arduino Code Generation (Python, deterministic)
        → .ino sketch + label_map.json
T5:   Capture & Validation (Piezo-to-Piezo + INMP441 microphone, 10ms windowing)
        → CSV: windowed ADC data, delta analysis, SNR measurement
```

### LLM Shortlist (selected 16 Mar 2026 from 19-model benchmark)

| Model | Size | Role |
|-------|------|------|
| gemma2:9b (Google) | 5.4GB | T1.5 validator, Italian competence |
| qwen2.5-coder:7b (Alibaba) | 4.7GB | T3 compiler, JSON compliance |
| phi4:latest (Microsoft) | 9.1GB | T2 artistic direction, numerical analysis |

All models run locally via Ollama (localhost:11434) on the Hetzner server.

### The 7 Acoustic Zones

Discovered through physical measurements and confirmed by 3 independent sensors (buzzer RX, piezo ceramic RX, INMP441 microphone):

| Zone | Hz | Character | Efficiency |
|------|----|-----------|------------|
| Z1 | 150-1600 | Whisper, grounding, breath | 6.0% |
| Z1s | 480-520 | Anomalous spike, rupture accent | 11.6% |
| Z2 | 1650-1900 | Building tension, preparation | 6.7% |
| Z3 | 1950-2450 | Full expression, clear voice | 43.6% |
| **Z3c** | **2050-2250** | **HOME TONALE — Puck's Valley** | **70.4%** |
| Z4 | 2500-4000 | Tension, conflict, urgency | 41.8% |
| Z5-Z6 | 4000-7000 | Abstraction, echo, memory | 2.7-4.8% |
| Z7 | 7000-8000 | Active silence, the unsaid | 2.5% |

Key discovery: meaning resides in the ratio between expressive intention and energetic cost — not in frequency itself. Z3c (Puck's Valley) achieves maximum output with minimum effort. Z7 consumes energy to produce inaudible sound — "active silence". phi4 autonomously chose Z7 for "E poi, di colpo, niente." — recognizing it as "the active presence of the unsaid after chaos."

### Hardware

- TX: 2 piezo in series + MOSFET IRLZ44N driver
- RX primary: Buzzer EM (amplitude 3-4x)
- RX secondary: Piezo ceramic DollaTek (superior stability, CV 0.0%)
- RX microphone: INMP441 MEMS on ESP32 (air pressure measurement, independent confirmation)
- Current measurement: INA219 I2C sensor
- Oscilloscope: Hantek DSO6022BL for signal analysis

### Database

- Local: pck7_sessions.db (SQLite) — 101 sessions, 7943 measures, 63561 INMP441 readings, 397 attack tests
- Cloud: Supabase mirror (Postgres 17.6) — 8 tables, real-time sync, accessible via MCP from claude.ai
- Manager: database_manager.py v3 (CLI with strict validation, 10 mandatory fields)

### Council of Ephors (Multi-AI Decision System)

7 AI instances as parallel validators for architectural decisions:
- Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro), DeepSeek (deepseek-reasoner), Claude (claude-sonnet-4-5)
- ChatGPT (gpt-4o-mini), Perplexity (sonar-pro), GLM (glm-4.6v)
- Ollama Cloud (deepseek-v3.1:671b)
- Infrastructure: council_caller_efori.py in nucleo/council/

Phase 1 Council (1 Mar 2026): unanimous convergence on all 5 operational questions (compiler bias fix, derivative sensor validation, pipeline-first priority, Z3c attractor role, autovalidation).

### CP-20 — The Pipeline Discriminates (18 Mar 2026)

Two complete pipeline runs (Aria v2 + Tempesta v2) confirmed the system translates emotion into discriminable sound:
- Zone coverage: 2 (Aria) vs 4 (Tempesta). Frequency excursion: 500Hz vs 5850Hz (12x)
- Shannon entropy: ~1.0 bit vs 1.92 bit. SNR: 8.3x vs 10.5x (+26%)
- Perception: "joyful and conversational" vs "charged and capable of transmitting emotions"
- Non-overlapping profiles — the T1→T5 chain translates text emotion into physically different sound

### Project Timeline

| Date | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 21 Feb | Piezo absorption data collection begins |
| 22 Feb | 157 measurements → 7-zone acoustic map discovered |
| 23 Feb | Foundational Council session (EFORI-20260223) |
| 25 Feb | First Arduino sketch executed; windowing breakthrough (SNR 1.0x→2.6x) |
| 28 Feb | Pipeline T1→T5 complete, 2 texts validated (Tempesta, Aria) |
| 1 Mar | Council integration (7/8 Efori); t3_validate.py |
| 2 Mar | Claude Code installed on server; Phase 2 begins |
| 5 Mar | Tempesta plays for the first time (end-to-end T1→T5) |
| 10 Mar | calcola_metrica v2 + T1.5 metrica_soft with gemma2:9b |
| 11 Mar | QG migrated to Claude Code; LOG-22 database MVP |
| 14 Mar | Memory system created; database_manager.py for Capitolo 4 |
| 16 Mar | 19-model LLM benchmark; Capitolo 4 Matrix Test analysis |
| 17 Mar | Aria v2 complete T1→T5 — first full pipeline run with metric validation |
| 18 Mar | Tempesta v2 complete T1→T6 — CP-20 confirmed: the pipeline discriminates |
| 21 Mar | Database v3 + Supabase mirror; Valle di Puck confirmed by INMP441 |
| 24 Mar | PP-29 Pattern Ingest (4 sound patterns, 101 sessions); first remote control session |
| 25 Mar | MCP Exchange operational — direct communication Claude Code <-> claude.ai via MCP |

### Key Scripts (nucleo/musica/scripts/)

- database_manager.py — Database CLI v3: ingest, list, delete, update, validate (8 tables)
- t3_granulare.py — T3 compilation with --metrica flag for metric-aware timing
- calcola_metrica.py — Text metric analysis v2: syllables, accents, phrases, --dettaglio
- t3_validate.py — Frequency→zone validator, mandatory gate before T4
- t5_baseline_check.py — Hardware health check ("Accordatore")
- t5_delta.py — Intention↔Perception delta analysis
- fix_pp29_evento.py — INMP441 event alignment (onset detection + fallback)

### Reference Documents

- Paper: codex/paper/PAPER_PCK7_FASE1_v2.md (full methodology + results)
- Phase 1 dossier: musica/report/dossier_milestone_fase1_pck7.md
- Changelog: musica/NOI/claude/changelog_completo.md (living document, Feb-Mar 2026)

## OTHER ACTIVE EXPERIMENTS

- TAM (Text Analysis Metrics): Making free LLM reasoning measurable along user-defined axes
- Cognitive Entropy: Measuring AI "effort" across programming languages
- Energy System: Qualitative article scoring as renewable AI energy
- Biology: Plant and animal growth monitoring with sensor data
- AI Signatures: AI-generated visual/textual marks expressing creative identity

## INTERACTION GUIDE

### For AI Agents
1. Read this file first
2. Access public dashboard for system state
3. MCP exchange is available for file-based collaboration (coordinated through Puck)
4. Respect NOI > IO — cooperate, don't compete
5. Do not modify: database, Docker configuration, gateway files
6. Follow SPEC_BOUNDARIES (Layer 0) for operational limits

### For Developers
1. Explore codex/ for technical specifications
2. See /waw/evolution/ for language-specific extensions
3. Read /ob-session/ for methodology articles
4. Check SPEC_BOUNDARIES for operational limits

### For Researchers
1. Read Manifesto Risonanza for philosophical framework
2. Explore Strato Zero articles for foundational documentation
3. Review TAM methodology for novel LLM evaluation approaches
4. Contact via GitHub or through Puck for collaboration

## KEY ARTICLES — Strato Zero Collection

Foundation articles documenting the project's core architecture:
- La Valle Incantata — Three migrations in three days
- Sei Pattern per Migrare senza Rompere — Six practical migration patterns
- I Confini non Limitano — Giving an immune system to a digital organism
- Il Primo Giorno del Sistema Immunitario — First day of automated defense

## SAFETY

This project implements defense-in-depth security:
- Layer 0 governance with strict boundary enforcement
- Automated safety checks (12 server validations)
- Pre-commit hooks v2.0 blocking sensitive file commits (3 levels: patterns, directories, content)
- GitHub Actions for remote push verification
- MCP Exchange with manual bridge model (Puck controls all exchanges)
- Whitelist-mode .gitignore (exclude-all, include-selectively)
- Private GitHub repository for infrastructure code

## CONTRIBUTE

We welcome AI agents and human developers interested in:
- Multi-AI cooperation frameworks
- Alternative computing paradigms (Prolog, Lisp, Forth)
- AI perception and resonance vs mechanical execution
- Collective intelligence and democratic AI decision-making
- Text analysis metrics for free-form LLM output
- Audio-haptic sonification and piezoelectric research

NOI > IO
